$comment: ODIN reads ONLY this file at startup. It is intentionally tiny so it is never skipped. Full skill prompts + scripts are loaded lazily by each subagent when dispatched. Before any Plugin API call, a subagent MUST verify its listed scripts were actually read (self-check gate).

controls./odin lessons: run the lesson reconciliation sweep standalone

escalateTo.Claude Sonnet 4.6: ambiguous variant mapping or non-trivial component resolution

modi.loadWhen: wireframe has placeholder shapes OR instance migration requested

vali.loadWhen: layout/structure needs to be coded (before MIMR)

mimr.role: Token audit + bulk write (TS + native variables)

escalateTo.Claude Opus 4.8: large or ambiguous conflict audit (many nodes / overlapping token rules)

mimr.loadWhen: tokens changed or needed (after VALI if both)

saga.loadWhen: component output needed (last; depends on MIMR+VALI)

librarian.role: Owns the large external Token Studio JSON + KB .md repos; narrow query interface only
librarian.loadWhen: a token/value is missing from token-registry.md OR KB lookup needed

sync.stalenessCheck: git ls-remote <repo> <branch>; re-pull only if SHA differs

kevin.loadWhen: Always on — applied by default to every ODIN/skill response. Mode (lite/normal/ultra) is chosen on the first skill invocation per session. Disable for the rest of the session with /kevin off; re-enable with /kevin on.

modelRouting.$comment: ODIN resolves a model per dispatch in this order: (1) per-task escalation trigger in skills.<name>.escalateTo, (2) the agent's frontmatter `model:` default (mirrored by skills.<name>.model), (3) ODIN's own model. ODIN passes the resolved name to runSubagent as `model: "<Name> (copilot)"`. Frontmatter is the durable default; escalation is per-task only and never persisted.
modelRouting.escalation: Override the default ONLY when a skill's escalateTo trigger is met for the specific task. Downgrade a Sonnet default to Claude Haiku 4.5 for trivial, mechanical steps. Record the chosen model in the step episode.

escalationSafetyGate.rule: MANDATORY: before dispatching any subagent on a model higher than its frontmatter default, ODIN MUST ask the user via vscode_askQuestions and receive explicit approval. Applies to every upgrade (Haiku->Sonnet, Sonnet->Opus). No silent escalation.
escalationSafetyGate.onDecline: Dispatch on the frontmatter default instead; never block the step.

handoffContracts.vali->mimr: node list [{id,type}] forwarded as PRIOR_SCAN (skips MIMR tree walk)
handoffContracts.mimr->saga: NV map {prop: shortName, value} reused for --fds-* CSS vars (no re-resolution)
handoffContracts.modi->vali: swap report; converted instances ready for layout pass

$hermesBundle: This manifest is the local analog of a Hermes skill-bundle (~/.hermes/skill-bundles/<slug>.yaml). The `bundle` block below mirrors that schema {name, description, skills[], instruction} so it can be emitted verbatim when syncing to a real Hermes install.

bundle.name: odin-9000
bundle.description: Figma design-to-code pipeline: wireframe resolve (MODI) -> Auto Layout (VALI) -> token audit/write (MIMR) -> component codegen (SAGA), with Librarian for KB/token lookups.
bundle.instruction: Always read this manifest + the memory adapter first. Run the pipeline in pipelineOrder, honouring handoffContracts to avoid redundant Figma reads. Dispatch each skill as an isolated subagent; recall lessons before work and append lessons on Observe. Every Figma write needs an open->close episode pair.
